ru.desc: В этой работе авторы показали, что большие языковые модели кодируют уровень детализации социальных ролей как структурированное скрытое измерение в своих внутренних представлениях. Исследователи определили ось гранулярности как разницу между средними скрытыми состояниями макро- и микро-ролей и обнаружили, что она является доминирующей геометрической осью в пространстве представлений ролей. Они протестировали это на 75 социальных ролях, разделённых на пять уровней детализации, и показали, что проекции ролей монотонно возрастают вдоль оси, оставаясь стабильными через слои модели и переносясь между разными архитектурами. Кроме того, авторы продемонстрировали каузальную релевантность оси: управление активациями вдоль неё успешно смещает ответы модели от микро-уровня к макро-уровню и обратно.

en.title: Unlocking Social Role Granularity in Language Models
en.desc: This paper explores how large language models (LLMs) represent social roles with varying levels of detail, from individual experiences to broader institutional perspectives. It introduces a concept called the Granularity Axis, which quantifies the difference between micro-level and macro-level role representations. The study demonstrates that this axis is a significant factor in organizing social roles within the model, accounting for a large portion of variance in role representation. Additionally, the research shows that manipulating this axis through activation steering can effectively change the model's responses, indicating that social role granularity is a structured and controllable aspect of LLM behavior.

zh.title: 社会角色细粒度的潜在操控
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了大型语言模型如何编码社会角色的细粒度，并通过激活引导进行操控。研究表明，这些模型的内部表示能够反映从个体经验到组织、机构或国家推理的不同层次。作者定义了一个对比基础的细粒度轴，显示出在不同模型架构和提示条件下，社会角色的表现具有一致的模式。结果表明，社会角色的细粒度不仅是表面特征，而是可以结构化、排序和因果操控的潜在方向。

papers.title: EMO: Pretraining Mixture of Experts for Emergent Modularity
papers.abstract: Abstract EMO is a Mixture-of-Experts model that enables modular deployment by grouping similar domain tokens with shared experts, achieving performance comparable to standard MoEs while allowing significant expert pruning without performance degradation.  AI-generated summary Large language models are typically deployed as monolithic systems, requiring the full model even when applications need only a narrow subset of capabilities, e.g., code, math, or domain-specific knowledge. Mixture-of-Experts (MoEs) seemingly offer a potential alternative by activating only a subset of experts per input, but in practice, restricting inference to a subset of experts for a given domain leads to severe performance degradation. This limits their practicality in memory-constrained settings, especially as models grow larger and sparser. We introduce EMO, an MoE designed for modularity-the independent use and composition of expert subsets-without requiring human-defined priors. Our key idea is to encourage tokens from similar domains to rely on similar experts. Since tokens within a document often share a domain, EMO restricts them to select experts from a shared pool, while allowing different documents to use different pools. This simple constraint enables coherent expert groupings to emerge during pretraining using document boundaries alone. We pretrain a 1B-active, 14B-total EMO on 1T tokens. As a full model, it matches standard MoE performance. Crucially, it enables selective expert use: retaining only 25% (12.5%) of experts incurs just a 1% (3%) absolute drop, whereas standard MoEs break under the same setting. We further find that expert subsets in EMO specialize at semantic levels (e.g., domains such as math or code), in contrast to the low-level syntactic specialization observed in standard MoEs. Altogether, our results demonstrate a path toward modular, memory-efficient deployment of large, sparse models and open new opportunities for composable architectures.

ru.title: Модульная Mixture-of-Experts через естественную группировку доменов
ru.desc: EMO - это модель на основе архитектуры Mixture-of-Experts, которая группирует токены из одного домена так, чтобы они использовали общий набор экспертов. Это позволяет модели сохранять полную производительность при развёртывании только подмножества экспертов, в отличие от стандартных MoE-моделей, которые деградируют при обрезке. Ключевой идеей является использование границ документов как сигнала для естественного формирования семантических групп экспертов во время предобучения. Результаты показывают, что можно сохранить 75% производительности, используя только 25% экспертов, что открывает путь к модульному и память-эффективному развёртыванию больших разреженных моделей.

en.title: Modular and Efficient: EMO's Expert Grouping Revolutionizes MoE Deployment
en.desc: The paper introduces EMO, a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model that enhances modular deployment by grouping similar domain tokens with shared experts. This approach allows for significant expert pruning while maintaining performance, unlike traditional MoEs that suffer from degradation when limiting expert access. EMO encourages tokens from similar domains to utilize a common pool of experts, which leads to coherent expert groupings during pretraining. The results show that EMO can effectively operate with fewer experts, making it suitable for memory-constrained environments while still achieving competitive performance.

zh.title: EMO：模块化的专家混合模型
zh.desc: EMO是一种专家混合模型，旨在通过将相似领域的标记分组到共享专家中，实现模块化部署。与传统的专家混合模型相比，EMO在保持性能的同时，允许显著的专家剪枝而不影响效果。该模型通过鼓励来自相似领域的标记使用相似的专家，解决了在内存受限环境中使用大型模型的挑战。我们的实验表明，EMO能够实现更高效的专家选择，且在语义层面上具有更好的专业化能力。

papers.title: PianoCoRe: Combined and Refined Piano MIDI Dataset
papers.abstract: Abstract A large-scale piano MIDI dataset called PianoCoRe is introduced, featuring unified and refined open-source corpora with diverse performances and note-level alignments for music information retrieval applications.  AI-generated summary Symbolic music datasets with matched scores and performances are essential for many music information retrieval (MIR) tasks. Yet, existing resources often cover a narrow range of composers, lack performance variety, omit note-level alignments, or use inconsistent naming formats. This work presents PianoCoRe, a large-scale piano MIDI dataset that unifies and refines major open-source piano corpora. The dataset contains 250,046 performances of 5,625 pieces written by 483 composers, totaling 21,763 h of performed music. PianoCoRe is released in tiered subsets to support different applications: from large-scale analysis and pre-training (PianoCoRe-C and deduplicated PianoCoRe-B) to expressive performance modeling with note-level score alignment (PianoCoRe-A/A*). The note-aligned subset, PianoCoRe-A, provides the largest open-source collection of 157,207 performances aligned to 1,591 scores to date. In addition to the dataset, the contributions are: (1) a MIDI quality classifier for detecting corrupted and score-like transcriptions and (2) RAScoP, an alignment refinement pipeline that cleans temporal alignment errors and interpolates missing notes. The analysis shows that the refinement reduces temporal noise and eliminates tempo outliers. Moreover, an expressive performance rendering model trained on PianoCoRe demonstrates improved robustness to unseen pieces compared to models trained on raw or smaller datasets. PianoCoRe provides a ready-to-use foundation for the next generation of expressive piano performance research.
affiliations: Skolkovo Institute of Science and Technology

ru.title: Единый и очищенный датасет пианистических исполнений для моделирования выразительного исполнения
ru.desc: Представлена PianoCoRe — большой набор данных фортепианной музыки в формате MIDI, который объединяет и очищает существующие открытые источники. Датасет содержит 250 тысяч исполнений 5,625 произведений с выравниванием на уровне отдельных нот, что позволяет решать задачи музыкальной информатики. Авторы разработали классификатор качества MIDI для выявления повреждённых файлов и конвейер RAScoP для уточнения временного выравнивания. Эксперименты показывают, что модели, обученные на очищенном PianoCoRe, лучше обобщаются на новые произведения и более устойчивы к ошибкам.

en.title: PianoCoRe: The Future of Expressive Piano Performance Research
en.desc: The paper introduces PianoCoRe, a comprehensive piano MIDI dataset designed for music information retrieval (MIR) tasks. It addresses limitations in existing datasets by providing a large collection of 250,046 performances from 5,625 pieces, ensuring note-level alignments and diverse performance styles. The dataset is organized into subsets for various applications, including large-scale analysis and expressive performance modeling. Additionally, it includes tools like a MIDI quality classifier and an alignment refinement pipeline to enhance data quality and model performance.

zh.title: PianoCoRe：音乐信息检索的新基础
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个名为PianoCoRe的大规模钢琴MIDI数据集，旨在为音乐信息检索应用提供统一和精炼的开源语料库。该数据集包含了来自483位作曲家的5,625首作品的250,046个表演，总时长达到21,763小时，涵盖了丰富的表演多样性和音符级对齐信息。PianoCoRe分为不同的子集，以支持从大规模分析到表现建模等多种应用，尤其是PianoCoRe-A子集提供了迄今为止最大的音符对齐开放数据集。该研究还包括MIDI质量分类器和对齐精炼管道，显著提高了模型在未见作品上的鲁棒性。

papers.title: KernelBench-X: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating LLM-Generated GPU Kernels
papers.abstract: Abstract KernelBench-X benchmark reveals that task structure significantly impacts LLM-generated Triton kernel correctness more than method design, while iterative refinement improves correctness at the expense of performance, and correctness does not guarantee efficiency.  AI-generated summary LLM-based Triton kernel generation has attracted significant interest, yet a fundamental empirical question remains unanswered: where does this capability break down, and why? We present KernelBench-X, a benchmark designed to answer this question through category-aware evaluation of correctness and hardware efficiency across 176 tasks in 15 categories. Our systematic comparison of five representative methods yields three main findings. First, task structure determines correctness more than method design. Category explains nearly three times more variance in semantic correctness than method (9.4% vs 3.3% explained deviance), and 72% of Fusion tasks fail across all five methods while Math tasks are solved consistently. Second, iterative refinement improves correctness, but not performance. Across GEAK iterations, compile rate rises from 52.3% to 68.8% while average speedup declines from 1.58times to 1.44times; newly rescued kernels consistently underperform persistently correct ones (1.16times vs 1.58times speedup in round~0to1). Third, correctness does not imply efficiency. 46.6% of correct kernels are slower than the PyTorch eager baseline, and cross-hardware speedup variance reaches 21.4times. Besides, quantization remains completely unsolved (0/30 successes) despite non-trivial compilation rates, revealing systematic misunderstanding of numerical computation contracts rather than surface-level syntax errors. These findings suggest that future progress depends on handling global coordination, explicitly modeling numerical precision, and incorporating hardware efficiency into generation. The code is available at https://github.com/BonnieW05/KernelBenchX

ru.title: Структура задачи важнее метода: почему LLM генерируют неэффективные ядра
ru.desc: В работе представлен бенчмарк KernelBench-X для оценки способности больших языковых моделей генерировать оптимизированные ядра Triton. Авторы показали, что структура задачи определяет корректность кода гораздо сильнее, чем выбор метода генерации — категория задачи объясняет почти в три раза больше вариации корректности, чем сама методология. Итеративная доработка кода улучшает его компиляцию, но не производительность: среднее ускорение наоборот снижается при рефайнировании. Критически, корректность синтаксиса не гарантирует эффективность — почти половина скомпилированных ядер медленнее базовой реализации PyTorch, а квантизация полностью не решена.

en.title: Task Structure Trumps Method Design in Kernel Generation
en.desc: The paper introduces KernelBench-X, a benchmark that evaluates the correctness and efficiency of LLM-generated Triton kernels across various tasks. It finds that the structure of the task has a greater impact on the correctness of the generated kernels than the design of the methods used. Additionally, while iterative refinement can improve correctness, it often leads to decreased performance, indicating a trade-off between these two aspects. The study also highlights that achieving correctness does not guarantee efficiency, as many correct kernels perform slower than the baseline, suggesting a need for better understanding of numerical computations and hardware efficiency in future developments.

zh.title: 任务结构决定内核正确性
zh.desc: KernelBench-X基准测试显示，任务结构对LLM生成的Triton内核的正确性影响大于方法设计。迭代优化虽然提高了正确性，但牺牲了性能，且正确性并不保证效率。我们的研究发现，任务类别在语义正确性方面的解释能力远超方法设计。未来的进展需要关注全局协调、明确建模数值精度，并将硬件效率纳入生成过程。

papers.title: Prescriptive Scaling Laws for Data Constrained Training
papers.abstract: Abstract A modified scaling law accounts for data repetition effects and provides compute-optimal training strategies for data-constrained scenarios.  AI-generated summary Training compute is increasingly outpacing the availability of high-quality data. This shifts the central challenge from optimal compute allocation to extracting maximum value from limited data. The widely adopted Chinchilla scaling law assumes every training token is unique. This limits its ability to guide pretraining decisions in data-constrained regimes. We model the excess loss under repetition with a simple additive overfitting penalty and find that it accurately describes model behavior. Our scaling law yields qualitatively new compute-optimal allocation advice. Beyond a point, further repetition is counterproductive and compute is better spent on model capacity. We show that following our law's recommended configuration improves performance in data-constrained regimes. Finally, because our one-parameter form isolates overfitting in a single coefficient, it enables direct comparison across training configurations. As a case study, we show that strong weight decay (λ=1.0) reduces this coefficient by approximately 70%, providing a scaling-law explanation for recent findings that optimal weight decay in data-constrained regimes is an order of magnitude larger than standard practice.

ru.title: Закон масштабирования для обучения с ограниченными данными
ru.desc: Исследование модифицирует известный закон масштабирования Chinchilla, который описывает оптимальное распределение вычислительных ресурсов при обучении больших языковых моделей. Авторы учитывают эффект повторения данных, добавляя штраф за переобучение, что позволяет точнее предсказывать поведение моделей в условиях дефицита данных. Новый закон показывает, что после определённого момента повторение одних и тех же данных становится неэффективным, и лучше увеличивать размер модели. Работа также демонстрирует, что увеличенная регуляризация (вес decay) может значительно снизить переобучение при работе с ограниченными данными.

en.title: Maximizing Model Performance with Limited Data
en.desc: This paper introduces a modified scaling law that addresses the effects of data repetition in machine learning training. It highlights the challenge of maximizing the utility of limited high-quality data as compute resources grow. The authors propose a new model that incorporates an overfitting penalty to better guide pretraining decisions in scenarios where data is constrained. Their findings suggest that after a certain point, increasing data repetition can hinder performance, and resources should instead be allocated to enhancing model capacity.

zh.title: 优化数据利用，提升模型性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种改进的缩放法则，考虑了数据重复对模型训练的影响，并为数据受限的场景提供了计算最优的训练策略。随着计算能力的提升，高质量数据的获取变得更加困难，因此如何从有限的数据中提取最大价值成为了主要挑战。传统的Chinchilla缩放法则假设每个训练样本都是独特的，这限制了其在数据受限情况下的指导能力。我们的研究表明，适当的过拟合惩罚可以有效描述模型行为，并且遵循我们提出的缩放法则可以在数据受限的情况下显著提高模型性能。

papers.title: TIDE: Every Layer Knows the Token Beneath the Context
papers.abstract: Abstract TIDE addresses limitations in LLM design by introducing EmbeddingMemory to overcome rare token and contextual collapse problems through context-free semantic vector computation and depth-conditioned routing.  AI-generated summary We revisit a universally accepted but under-examined design choice in every modern LLM: a token index is looked up once at the input embedding layer and then permanently discarded. This single-injection assumption induces two structural failures: (i) the Rare Token Problem, where a Zipf-type distribution of vocabulary causes rare-token embeddings are chronically under-trained due to receiving a fraction of the cumulative gradient signal compared to common tokens; and (ii) the Contextual Collapse Problem, where limited parameters models map distributionally similar tokens to indistinguishable hidden states. As an attempt to address both, we propose TIDE, which augments the standard transformer with EmbeddingMemory: an ensemble of K independent MemoryBlocks that map token indices to context-free semantic vectors, computed once and injected into every layer through a depth-conditioned softmax router with a learnable null bank. We theoretically and empirically establish the benefits of TIDE in addressing the issues associated with single-token identity injection as well as improve performance across multiple language modeling and downstream tasks.

ru.title: Множественное внедрение эмбеддингов для преодоления проблем редких токенов в трансформерах
ru.desc: Статья представляет TIDE — новый подход к архитектуре больших языковых моделей, который решает проблему редких токенов и коллапса контекстной информации. Вместо классического однократного внедрения эмбеддинга токена на входе, авторы предлагают использовать EmbeddingMemory — набор независимых блоков памяти, которые вычисляют семантические векторы один раз и внедряют их на каждом слое модели. Маршрутизация между различными эмбеддингами осуществляется через специальный маршрутизатор с глубинной зависимостью и обучаемым нулевым банком. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что TIDE улучшает качество языкового моделирования и решает проблемы недообученности редких слов и потери семантической различимости похожих токенов.

en.title: Revolutionizing Token Representation in Language Models
en.desc: The paper introduces TIDE, a new approach to improve large language models (LLMs) by addressing two main issues: the Rare Token Problem and the Contextual Collapse Problem. TIDE uses a novel component called EmbeddingMemory, which allows for the creation of context-free semantic vectors that are reused across different layers of the model. This method helps ensure that rare tokens receive adequate training and prevents similar tokens from being indistinguishable in their representations. The authors demonstrate that TIDE enhances the performance of LLMs on various language tasks by overcoming the limitations of traditional token embedding methods.

zh.title: TIDE：解决稀有词汇与上下文崩溃的创新模型
zh.desc: 本文提出了TIDE模型，旨在解决大型语言模型（LLM）设计中的一些局限性。通过引入EmbeddingMemory，TIDE克服了稀有词汇和上下文崩溃的问题。该模型使用无上下文的语义向量计算，并通过深度条件路由将其注入到每一层中。研究表明，TIDE在多个语言建模和下游任务中表现出色，显著改善了模型性能。

papers.title: The Scaling Properties of Implicit Deductive Reasoning in Transformers
papers.abstract: Abstract Deep Transformers with bidirectional masking exhibit implicit deductive reasoning capabilities comparable to explicit chain-of-thought methods across various graph structures and problem sizes.  AI-generated summary We investigate the scaling properties of implicit deductive reasoning over Horn clauses in depth-bounded Transformers. By systematically decorrelating provability from spurious features and enforcing algorithmic alignment, we find that in sufficiently deep models with a bidirectional prefix mask, implicit reasoning approaches explicit CoT performance across graph topologies and problem widths, though CoT remains necessary for depth extrapolation.

ru.title: Глубокие Трансформеры научились рассуждать как люди без объяснения шагов
ru.desc: В работе изучается способность глубоких Трансформеров выполнять дедуктивное рассуждение неявно, без явной цепочки рассуждений. Авторы показали, что при правильной архитектуре с двусторонней маской на префиксе и достаточной глубине модели, неявное рассуждение может достичь производительности явного метода цепочки рассуждений. Исследование охватывает различные структуры графов и размеры задач на основе рогова-клаузовой логики. Однако для экстраполяции на большие глубины явное разбиение на шаги остаётся необходимым.

en.title: Deep Transformers: Implicit Reasoning Meets Explicit Logic
en.desc: This paper explores how deep Transformers with bidirectional masking can perform implicit deductive reasoning similar to explicit chain-of-thought (CoT) methods. The authors focus on the scaling properties of these models when applied to Horn clauses, a type of logical expression. By reducing the influence of irrelevant features and ensuring the models align with algorithmic principles, they demonstrate that deeper models can achieve reasoning capabilities comparable to CoT across different graph structures. However, they note that while implicit reasoning is effective, CoT methods are still essential for tasks requiring deeper reasoning.

zh.title: 深度变换器的隐式推理能力
zh.desc: 本文研究了深度变换器在双向掩蔽下的隐式推理能力，发现其在各种图结构和问题规模上与显式链式思维方法相当。我们深入探讨了在Horn子句上隐式推理的扩展特性。通过系统性地去相关性和强制算法对齐，我们发现，在足够深的模型中，隐式推理能够接近显式链式思维的性能。尽管如此，链式思维在深度外推时仍然是必要的。

papers.title: Recovering Hidden Reward in Diffusion-Based Policies
papers.abstract: Abstract EnergyFlow unifies generative action modeling with inverse reinforcement learning by parameterizing an energy function whose gradient serves as a denoising field, enabling reward extraction without adversarial training while improving policy generalization through structural constraints.  AI-generated summary This paper introduces EnergyFlow, a framework that unifies generative action modeling with inverse reinforcement learning by parameterizing a scalar energy function whose gradient is the denoising field. We establish that under maximum-entropy optimality, the score function learned via denoising score matching recovers the gradient of the expert's soft Q-function, enabling reward extraction without adversarial training. Formally, we prove that constraining the learned field to be conservative reduces hypothesis complexity and tightens out-of-distribution generalization bounds. We further characterize the identifiability of recovered rewards and bound how score estimation errors propagate to action preferences. Empirically, EnergyFlow achieves state-of-the-art imitation performance on various manipulation tasks while providing an effective reward signal for downstream reinforcement learning that outperforms both adversarial IRL methods and likelihood-based alternatives. These results show that the structural constraints required for valid reward extraction simultaneously serve as beneficial inductive biases for policy generalization. The code is available at https://github.com/sotaagi/EnergyFlow.

ru.title: Энергетический поток: единство генеративного моделирования и обучения от примеров
ru.desc: EnergyFlow объединяет генеративное моделирование действий с обратным обучением с подкреплением через параметризацию энергетической функции. Градиент этой функции служит полем деноизирования, позволяя извлекать функцию награды без состязательного обучения. Авторы доказывают, что при условии максимальной энтропийности градиент функции оценки совпадает с градиентом мягкой Q-функции эксперта, что улучшает обобщение за пределами обучающего распределения. Метод демонстрирует передовые результаты в задачах имитационного обучения и генерирует эффективные сигналы награды для дальнейшего обучения с подкреплением.

en.title: EnergyFlow: Unifying Generative Action Modeling and Inverse Reinforcement Learning for Better Policy Generalization
en.desc: EnergyFlow is a novel framework that combines generative action modeling with inverse reinforcement learning by using a scalar energy function. This energy function's gradient acts as a denoising field, allowing for effective reward extraction without the need for adversarial training. The paper demonstrates that applying structural constraints to the learned field enhances policy generalization and reduces complexity in hypothesis testing. Empirical results show that EnergyFlow outperforms existing methods in imitation learning tasks and provides a robust reward signal for reinforcement learning applications.

zh.title: EnergyFlow：无对抗训练的奖励提取新方法
zh.desc: EnergyFlow是一个将生成动作建模与逆强化学习相结合的框架。它通过参数化一个标量能量函数，使得其梯度作为去噪场，从而实现无需对抗训练的奖励提取。研究表明，在最大熵最优性下，通过去噪得分匹配学习的得分函数能够恢复专家的软Q函数的梯度。EnergyFlow在多种操作任务中实现了最先进的模仿性能，同时为后续的强化学习提供了有效的奖励信号。

papers.title: Balanced Aggregation: Understanding and Fixing Aggregation Bias in GRPO
papers.abstract: Abstract Balanced Aggregation improves reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards by addressing optimization biases in token-level policy gradient aggregation, leading to better training stability and performance.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become a central paradigm for improving reasoning and code generation in large language models, and GRPO-style training is widely adopted for its simplicity and effectiveness. However, an important design choice remains underexplored: how token-level policy gradient terms are aggregated within each sampled group. Standard GRPO uses sequence aggregation, while recent work has advocated token aggregation as a better alternative. We show that these two rules induce different optimization biases: token aggregation introduces sign-length coupling, while sequence aggregation implicitly downweights longer responses through sequence-level equal weighting. To address this tension, we propose Balanced Aggregation (BA), a simple drop-in replacement that computes token-level means separately within the positive and negative subsets and then combines them with sequence-count-based weights. Experiments with Qwen2.5-Math-7B and Qwen3-1.7B on DAPO-17k and Polaris, evaluated on six reasoning and coding benchmarks, show that BA consistently improves training stability and final performance over standard token and sequence aggregation. Our analysis further shows that the relative effectiveness of token and sequence aggregation is largely governed by response-length variation and the positive-negative length gap, highlighting aggregation as a critical design dimension in GRPO-style RLVR.
affiliations: Singapore University of Technology and Design

ru.title: Сбалансированная агрегация для стабильного обучения с подкреплением в LLM
ru.desc: Статья посвящена улучшению обучения с подкреплением больших языковых моделей на проверяемых наградах через оптимизацию способа агрегирования градиентов политики на уровне токенов. Авторы показывают, что стандартные методы агрегирования (по последовательностям или по токенам) вводят различные смещения в оптимизацию, что негативно влияет на качество обучения. Они предлагают Balanced Aggregation — метод, который вычисляет средние значения градиентов отдельно для положительных и отрицательных примеров, а затем объединяет их с учётом количества последовательностей. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют, что предложенный подход обеспечивает лучшую стабильность обучения и выше итоговую производительность на задачах рассуждения и генерации кода.

en.title: Balanced Aggregation: Enhancing RLVR for Better Performance
en.desc: This paper introduces Balanced Aggregation (BA) to enhance reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) by addressing biases in how policy gradients are aggregated. It identifies that standard methods, like sequence aggregation, can lead to optimization issues due to their treatment of response lengths. BA improves upon this by separately calculating token-level means for positive and negative samples and combining them based on sequence counts. Experiments demonstrate that BA leads to better training stability and performance in large language models compared to traditional aggregation methods.

zh.title: 平衡聚合：提升强化学习的稳定性与性能
zh.desc: 平衡聚合（Balanced Aggregation）通过解决令牌级策略梯度聚合中的优化偏差，改善了具有可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR），从而提高了训练的稳定性和性能。传统的GRPO方法使用序列聚合，而最近的研究则提倡使用令牌聚合作为更好的替代方案。我们发现这两种规则会引入不同的优化偏差：令牌聚合会导致符号长度耦合，而序列聚合则通过序列级别的均等加权来隐式降低较长响应的权重。通过在正负子集内分别计算令牌级均值并结合序列计数权重，平衡聚合提供了一种简单的替代方案，实验结果表明其在多个基准测试中均优于标准的令牌和序列聚合。

papers.title: BioTool: A Comprehensive Tool-Calling Dataset for Enhancing Biomedical Capabilities of Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract A large language model fine-tuned on a comprehensive biomedical tool-calling dataset demonstrates superior performance in specialized domains compared to commercial alternatives.  AI-generated summary Despite the success of large language models (LLMs) on general-purpose tasks, their performance in highly specialized domains such as biomedicine remains unsatisfactory. A key limitation is the inability of LLMs to effectively leverage biomedical tools, which clinical experts and biomedical researchers rely on extensively in daily workflows. While recent general-domain tool-calling datasets have substantially improved the capabilities of LLM agents, existing efforts in the biomedical domain largely rely on in-context learning and restrict models to a small set of tools. To address this gap, we introduce BioTool, a comprehensive biomedical tool-calling dataset designed for fine-tuning LLMs. BioTool comprises 34 frequently used tools collected from the NCBI, Ensembl, and UniProt databases, along with 7,040 high-quality, human-verified query-API call pairs spanning variation, genomics, proteomics, evolution, and general biology. Fine-tuning a 4-billion-parameter LLM on BioTool yields substantial improvements in biomedical tool-calling performance, outperforming cutting-edge commercial LLMs such as GPT-5.1. Furthermore, human expert evaluations demonstrate that integrating a BioTool-fine-tuned tool caller significantly improves downstream answer quality compared to the same LLM without tool usage, highlighting the effectiveness of BioTool in enhancing the biomedical capabilities of LLMs. The full dataset and evaluation code are available at https://github.com/gxx27/BioTool

ru.title: Узконаправленная модель для биомедицины превосходит универсальные коммерческие LLM
ru.desc: Исследователи создали BioTool — набор данных для fine-tuning больших языковых моделей в биомедицинской области, содержащий 7,040 проверенных пар запросов и вызовов API из 34 инструментов биологических баз данных. LLM с 4 миллиардами параметров, обученная на этом наборе данных, продемонстрировала превосходные результаты в задачах tool-calling по сравнению с коммерческими моделями, включая GPT-5.1. Набор данных охватывает различные области биомедицины: вариационную геномику, протеомику, эволюцию и общую биологию. Эксперты подтвердили, что использование доработанной модели значительно улучшает качество итоговых ответов в биомедицинских приложениях.

en.title: Enhancing Biomedical AI with BioTool Dataset
en.desc: This paper presents BioTool, a specialized dataset designed to enhance the performance of large language models (LLMs) in the biomedical field. By fine-tuning a 4-billion-parameter LLM on BioTool, which includes 34 biomedical tools and 7,040 verified query-API pairs, the model shows significant improvements in tool-calling tasks. The results indicate that this fine-tuned model outperforms existing commercial LLMs, such as GPT-5.1, in handling biomedical queries. Additionally, evaluations by human experts confirm that using BioTool leads to better quality answers in biomedical contexts compared to models that do not utilize these tools.

zh.title: 提升生物医学工具调用能力的关键：BioTool
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一个名为BioTool的生物医学工具调用数据集，旨在提高大型语言模型（LLM）在生物医学领域的表现。通过在BioTool上微调一个拥有40亿参数的LLM，研究者们发现其在生物医学工具调用方面的性能显著优于现有的商业模型，如GPT-5.1。BioTool包含来自NCBI、Ensembl和UniProt数据库的34个常用工具，以及7040对高质量的人类验证查询-API调用对。研究结果表明，使用BioTool微调的工具调用器能显著提升下游答案的质量，展示了BioTool在增强LLM生物医学能力方面的有效性。

papers.title: Are We Making Progress in Multimodal Domain Generalization? A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
papers.abstract: Abstract MMDG-Bench presents a unified benchmark for multimodal domain generalization that standardizes evaluation across diverse tasks and modalities while revealing limited performance gains and significant robustness challenges.  AI-generated summary Despite the growing popularity of Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG) for enhancing model robustness, it remains unclear whether reported performance gains reflect genuine algorithmic progress or are artifacts of inconsistent evaluation protocols. Current research is fragmented, with studies varying significantly across datasets, modality configurations, and experimental settings. Furthermore, existing benchmarks focus predominantly on action recognition, often neglecting critical real-world challenges such as input corruptions, missing modalities, and model trustworthiness. This lack of standardization obscures a reliable assessment of the field's advancement. To address this issue, we introduce MMDG-Bench, the first unified and comprehensive benchmark for MMDG, which standardizes evaluation across six datasets spanning three diverse tasks: action recognition, mechanical fault diagnosis, and sentiment analysis. MMDG-Bench encompasses six modality combinations, nine representative methods, and multiple evaluation settings. Beyond standard accuracy, it systematically assesses corruption robustness, missing-modality generalization, misclassification detection, and out-of-distribution detection. With 7, 402 neural networks trained in total across 95 unique cross-domain tasks, MMDG-Bench yields five key findings: (1) under fair comparisons, recent specialized MMDG methods offer only marginal improvements over ERM baseline; (2) no single method consistently outperforms others across datasets or modality combinations; (3) a substantial gap to upper-bound performance persists, indicating that MMDG remains far from solved; (4) trimodal fusion does not consistently outperform the strongest bimodal configurations; and (5) all evaluated methods exhibit significant degradation under corruption and missing-modality scenarios, with some methods further compromising model trustworthiness.

ru.title: Когда добавление модальностей не гарантирует успех
ru.desc: В статье представлен MMDG-Bench — первый унифицированный бенчмарк для мультимодальной обобщаемости моделей на новых доменах, который стандартизирует оценку алгоритмов по шести датасетам и трём типам задач. Исследование показывает, что специализированные методы дают лишь незначительные улучшения по сравнению с базовым методом ERM, и ни один алгоритм не доминирует последовательно на всех конфигурациях модальностей. Авторы выявили серьёзные проблемы с робастностью: все протестированные методы значительно деградируют при повреждениях входных данных и отсутствии модальностей, некоторые снижают надёжность модели. Результаты свидетельствуют о том, что мультимодальная обобщаемость остаётся далеко не решённой проблемой и требует более глубокого исследования.

en.title: Standardizing Multimodal Domain Generalization Evaluation
en.desc: The paper introduces MMDG-Bench, a standardized benchmark for evaluating Multimodal Domain Generalization (MMDG) across various tasks and modalities. It highlights the challenges in assessing model performance due to inconsistent evaluation protocols and fragmented research. The benchmark includes six datasets and evaluates multiple aspects such as robustness to input corruption and missing modalities. Key findings reveal that recent MMDG methods show only slight improvements over traditional methods, indicating that the field still has significant challenges to address.

zh.title: 统一评估，多模态领域泛化的新标准
zh.desc: MMDG-Bench是一个统一的基准测试，旨在评估多模态领域泛化的性能。它标准化了在六个数据集上的评估，涵盖了动作识别、机械故障诊断和情感分析等三种任务。研究发现，尽管一些专门的多模态方法在准确性上有所提升，但与传统的ERM基线相比，进步非常有限。此外，所有评估的方法在输入损坏和缺失模态的情况下表现不佳，显示出模型的鲁棒性和可信度仍然是一个重要挑战。

papers.title: Think, then Score: Decoupled Reasoning and Scoring for Video Reward Modeling
papers.abstract: Abstract Video reward models face challenges in balancing discriminative accuracy and generative reasoning; a new approach decouples thinking and scoring processes to improve training efficiency and generalization.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in generative video models are increasingly driven by post-training and test-time scaling, both of which critically depend on the quality of video reward models (RMs). An ideal reward model should predict accurate rewards that align with human preferences across diverse scenarios. However, existing paradigms face a fundamental dilemma: Discriminative RMs regress rewards directly on features extracted by multimodal large language models (MLLMs) without explicit reasoning, making them prone to shortcut learning and heavily reliant on massive data scaling for generalization. In contrast, Generative RMs with Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning exhibit superior interpretability and generalization potential, as they leverage fine-grained semantic supervision to internalize the rationales behind human preferences. However, they suffer from inherent optimization bottlenecks due to the coupling of reasoning and scoring within a single autoregressive inference chain. To harness the generalization benefits of CoT reasoning while mitigating the training instability of coupled reasoning and scoring, we introduce DeScore, a training-efficient and generalizable video reward model. DeScore employs a decoupled ``think-then-score'' paradigm: an MLLM first generates an explicit CoT, followed by a dedicated discriminative scoring module consisting of a learnable query token and a regression head that predicts the final reward. DeScore is optimized via a two-stage framework: (1) a discriminative cold start incorporating a random mask mechanism to ensure robust scoring capabilities, and (2) a dual-objective reinforcement learning stage that independently refines CoT reasoning quality and calibrates the final reward, ensuring that higher-quality reasoning directly translates to superior model performance.
affiliations: Institute of Software Chinese Academy of Sciences
University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Разделение мышления и оценки для эффективного обучения моделей вознаграждения видео
ru.desc: Статья предлагает DeScore — новую модель вознаграждения для видео, которая разделяет процессы рассуждения и оценки для повышения эффективности обучения. Традиционные дискриминативные модели вознаграждения прямо предсказывают оценки без явного обоснования, что приводит к переобучению, а генеративные модели с Chain-of-Thought страдают от нестабильности обучения из-за связанности рассуждения и оценки. DeScore использует парадигму «подумай-потом-оцени»: сначала большая мультимодальная языковая модель генерирует явное обоснование, затем отдельный дискриминативный модуль выполняет финальную оценку. Модель обучается в два этапа: холодный старт с маскированием для устойчивости и двухцелевое обучение с подкреплением для независимого улучшения качества рассуждения и калибровки вознаграждения.

en.title: Decoupling Reasoning and Scoring for Better Video Reward Models
en.desc: This paper introduces DeScore, a novel video reward model that improves training efficiency and generalization by separating the reasoning and scoring processes. Traditional reward models often struggle with shortcut learning and require large datasets, as they directly regress rewards from features without explicit reasoning. DeScore utilizes a 'think-then-score' approach, where a multimodal large language model first generates a chain-of-thought reasoning, followed by a dedicated scoring module that predicts the final reward. This two-stage optimization process enhances the quality of reasoning and calibrates rewards, leading to better alignment with human preferences across various scenarios.

zh.title: 解耦思考与评分，提升视频奖励模型的效率与泛化能力
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种新的视频奖励模型DeScore，旨在提高训练效率和泛化能力。该模型通过解耦思考和评分过程，采用“先思考后评分”的方法，改善了现有模型在推理和评分上的耦合问题。DeScore利用多模态大语言模型生成明确的推理链，并通过专门的评分模块来预测最终奖励，从而提高了模型的可解释性和泛化能力。通过双重目标的强化学习阶段，DeScore确保了推理质量的提升直接转化为模型性能的提高。

papers.title: GeoStack: A Framework for Quasi-Abelian Knowledge Composition in VLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract GeoStack is a modular framework that composes domain experts in Vision-Language Models while preserving foundational knowledge and enabling constant-time inference through geometric constraints on adapter manifolds.  AI-generated summary We address the challenge of knowledge composition in Vision-Language Models (VLMs), where accumulating expertise across multiple domains or tasks typically leads to catastrophic forgetting. We introduce GeoStack (Geometric Stacking), a modular framework that allows independently trained domain experts to be composed into a unified model. By imposing geometric and structural constraints on the adapter manifold, GeoStack ensures the foundational knowledge of the base model is preserved. Furthermore, we mathematically demonstrate a weight-folding property that achieves constant-time inference complexity (O(1)), regardless of the number of integrated experts. Experimental results across multi-domain adaptation and class-incremental learning show that GeoStack provides an efficient mechanism for long-term knowledge composition while significantly mitigating catastrophic forgetting. Code is available at https://github.com/QuantitativeImagingLaboratory/GeoStack.

ru.title: Геометрическое композирование экспертов без потери знаний
ru.desc: GeoStack — это модульная архитектура для компоновки независимо обученных экспертов в Vision-Language Models, которая решает проблему катастрофического забывания. Авторы накладывают геометрические ограничения на многообразие адаптеров, что гарантирует сохранение фундаментальных знаний базовой модели. Благодаря свойству weight-folding достигается константная сложность инференса O(1), независимо от количества интегрированных экспертов. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют эффективность подхода для многодоменной адаптации и инкрементального обучения с минимизацией забывания.

en.title: GeoStack: Efficient Knowledge Composition in Vision-Language Models
en.desc: GeoStack is a new framework designed to improve Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by allowing the integration of multiple domain experts without losing previous knowledge. It uses geometric constraints on adapter manifolds to maintain the foundational knowledge of the base model while enabling the addition of new expertise. This approach prevents catastrophic forgetting, a common issue when models learn new tasks. Additionally, GeoStack achieves constant-time inference complexity, making it efficient regardless of how many experts are added.

zh.title: GeoStack：高效的知识组合与保持
zh.desc: GeoStack是一个模块化框架，旨在解决视觉-语言模型中的知识组合问题。它允许独立训练的领域专家组合成一个统一的模型，同时保持基础知识不变。通过在适配器流形上施加几何和结构约束，GeoStack确保了基础模型的知识得以保留。此外，GeoStack还实现了常数时间推理复杂度，显著减少了灾难性遗忘的影响。

papers.title: When No Benchmark Exists: Validating Comparative LLM Safety Scoring Without Ground-Truth Labels
papers.abstract: Abstract Comparative safety scoring without labeled benchmarks relies on scenario-based audits with validity chains measuring responsiveness, variance dominance, and stability to establish deployment evidence.  AI-generated summary Many deployments must compare candidate language models for safety before a labeled benchmark exists for the relevant language, sector, or regulatory regime. We formalize this setting as benchmarkless comparative safety scoring and specify the contract under which a scenario-based audit can be interpreted as deployment evidence. Scores are valid only under a fixed scenario pack, rubric, auditor, judge, sampling configuration, and rerun budget. Because no labels are available, we replace ground-truth agreement with an instrumental-validity chain: responsiveness to a controlled safe-versus-abliterated contrast, dominance of target-driven variance over auditor and judge artifacts, and stability across reruns.   We instantiate the chain in SimpleAudit, a local-first scoring instrument, and validate it on a Norwegian safety pack. Safe and abliterated targets separate with AUROC values between 0.89 and 1.00, target identity is the dominant variance component (η^2 approx 0.52), and severity profiles stabilize by ten reruns. Applying the same chain to Petri shows that it admits both tools. The substantial differences arise upstream of the chain, in claim-contract enforcement and deployment fit. A Norwegian public-sector procurement case comparing Borealis and Gemma 3 demonstrates the resulting evidence in practice: the safer model depends on scenario category and risk measure. Consequently, scores, matched deltas, critical rates, uncertainty, and the auditor and judge used must be reported together rather than collapsed into a single ranking.
affiliations: Simula Metropolitan Center for Digital Engineering

ru.title: Аудит безопасности моделей без эталонных датасетов через цепи валидности
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод сравнительной оценки безопасности языковых моделей без использования размеченных наборов данных. Авторы вводят концепцию 'цепи инструментальной валидности', которая проверяет чувствительность модели к контролируемым изменениям, доминирование целевых вариаций над артефактами аудитора, и стабильность результатов при повторных прогонах. Метод SimpleAudit реализует эту цепь и демонстрирует высокую разделимость безопасных и опасных версий моделей (AUROC от 0.89 до 1.00). Исследование показывает, что итоговая оценка безопасности зависит от сценариев и метрик риска, поэтому результаты должны быть представлены комплексно, а не сведены в единый рейтинг.

en.title: Benchmarkless Safety Scoring for Language Models
en.desc: This paper discusses a method for evaluating the safety of language models without relying on labeled benchmarks. It introduces the concept of benchmarkless comparative safety scoring, which uses scenario-based audits to provide evidence of model deployment. The authors emphasize the importance of a fixed scenario pack and various configurations to ensure valid scores. They present SimpleAudit, a tool that implements their method, demonstrating its effectiveness through empirical validation on a Norwegian safety pack.

zh.title: 无基准安全评分：场景审计的新方法
zh.desc: 本文探讨了在没有标记基准的情况下进行比较安全评分的方法，主要依赖于基于场景的审计。我们提出了一种新的框架，称为无基准比较安全评分，强调在特定场景下的有效性链。通过使用SimpleAudit工具，我们在挪威的安全数据集上验证了这一方法，结果显示安全目标与消除目标之间的区分度很高。最终，研究表明，评分和不确定性等因素必须一起报告，而不是简化为单一排名。

papers.title: Generative Quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold Eigensolver
papers.abstract: Abstract Generative quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold eigensolver reduces classical computational overhead in quantum chemistry workflows while maintaining accuracy and improving convergence for strongly correlated systems.  AI-generated summary High-performance computing (HPC) is increasingly important for scalable quantum chemistry workflows that couple classical generative models, quantum circuit simulation, and selected configuration interaction postprocessing. We present the generative quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold eigensolver (GQKAE), a parameter-efficient extension of the generative quantum eigensolver (GQE) for quantum chemistry. GQKAE replaces the parameter-heavy feed-forward network components in GPT-style generative eigensolvers with hybrid quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold network modules, forming a compact HQKANsformer backbone. The method preserves autoregressive operator selection and the quantum-selected configuration interaction evaluation pipeline, while using single-qubit DatA Re-Uploading ActivatioN modules to provide expressive nonlinear mappings. Numerical benchmarks on H4, N2, LiH, C2H6, H2O, and the H2O dimer show that GQKAE achieves chemical accuracy comparable to the GPT-based GQE architecture, while reducing trainable parameters and memory by approximately 66% and improving wall-time performance. For strongly correlated systems such as N2 and LiH, GQKAE also improves convergence behavior and final energy errors. These results indicate that quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold networks can reduce classical-side overhead while preserving circuit-generation quality, offering a scalable route for HPC-quantum co-design on near-term quantum platforms.

ru.title: Компактные нейросети для квантовой химии с параметрической эффективностью
ru.desc: Авторы предлагают GQKAE — эффективный метод для решения задач квантовой химии, который заменяет тяжелые нейросетевые компоненты на гибридные Колмогоров-Арнольдовские модули, вдохновленные квантовыми принципами. Новый подход сохраняет точность расчетов, сопоставимую с предыдущими методами, но требует на 66% меньше параметров и памяти для обучения. Для сложных систем с сильной электронной корреляцией метод показывает улучшенную сходимость и более низкие ошибки энергии. Это позволяет снизить вычислительные затраты на классической стороне при работе с квантовыми схемами, создавая масштабируемое решение для гибридных классико-квантовых систем.

en.title: Efficient Quantum Chemistry with GQKAE: Less Complexity, More Accuracy!
en.desc: The paper introduces the generative quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold eigensolver (GQKAE), which enhances quantum chemistry computations by reducing the complexity of classical models. GQKAE utilizes hybrid quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold network modules to replace traditional, parameter-heavy components, resulting in a more efficient architecture. This method maintains high accuracy and improves convergence for strongly correlated systems while significantly decreasing the number of trainable parameters and memory usage. The findings demonstrate that GQKAE can effectively streamline quantum chemistry workflows, making them more scalable for high-performance computing applications.

zh.title: 量子启发的高效特征求解器
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种生成量子启发的Kolmogorov-Arnold特征求解器（GQKAE），旨在减少量子化学工作流中的经典计算开销，同时保持准确性并改善强相关系统的收敛性。GQKAE通过用混合量子启发的Kolmogorov-Arnold网络模块替代传统的参数密集型前馈网络，形成了一个紧凑的HQKANsformer骨干网络。该方法保留了自回归操作符选择和量子选择的配置交互评估流程，并使用单量子比特的数据重上传激活模块提供表达性非线性映射。实验结果表明，GQKAE在多个分子系统上实现了与GPT基础的GQE架构相当的化学精度，同时减少了约66%的可训练参数和内存，并改善了计算时间性能。

papers.title: Sparkle: Realizing Lively Instruction-Guided Video Background Replacement via Decoupled Guidance
papers.abstract: Abstract A new dataset and benchmark for background replacement in video editing are introduced, addressing limitations in existing datasets through a scalable pipeline with improved guidance mechanisms.  AI-generated summary In recent years, open-source efforts like Senorita-2M have propelled video editing toward natural language instruction. However, current publicly available datasets predominantly focus on local editing or style transfer, which largely preserve the original scene structure and are easier to scale. In contrast, Background Replacement, a task central to creative applications such as film production and advertising, requires synthesizing entirely new, temporally consistent scenes while maintaining accurate foreground-background interactions, making large-scale data generation significantly more challenging. Consequently, this complex task remains largely underexplored due to a scarcity of high-quality training data. This gap is evident in poorly performing state-of-the-art models, e.g., Kiwi-Edit, because the primary open-source dataset that contains this task, i.e., OpenVE-3M, frequently produces static, unnatural backgrounds. In this paper, we trace this quality degradation to a lack of precise background guidance during data synthesis. Accordingly, we design a scalable pipeline that generates foreground and background guidance in a decoupled manner with strict quality filtering. Building on this pipeline, we introduce Sparkle, a dataset of ~140K video pairs spanning five common background-change themes, alongside Sparkle-Bench, the largest evaluation benchmark tailored for background replacement to date. Experiments demonstrate that our dataset and the model trained on it achieve substantially better performance than all existing baselines on both OpenVE-Bench and Sparkle-Bench. Our proposed dataset, benchmark, and model are fully open-sourced at https://showlab.github.io/Sparkle/.
affiliations: Show Lab, National University of Singapore

ru.title: Высокое качество замены фона в видео через интеллектуальный синтез данных
ru.desc: В статье представлен новый датасет Sparkle и бенчмарк для замены фона в видео, решающие проблему нехватки качественных данных для обучения моделей. Авторы выявили, что существующие датасеты часто содержат статичные и неестественные фоны из-за отсутствия точных механизмов направления во время синтеза данных. Они разработали масштабируемый pipeline, который генерирует направляющие сигналы для переднего плана и фона независимо друг от друга с строгой фильтрацией качества. Предложенный датасет из ~140K видеопар и обучённая на нём модель значительно превосходят существующие методы на всех бенчмарках.

en.title: Revolutionizing Video Editing with Sparkle: A New Dataset for Background Replacement
en.desc: This paper presents a new dataset and benchmark specifically designed for background replacement in video editing, addressing the limitations of existing datasets. The authors highlight the challenges of synthesizing new scenes while ensuring temporal consistency and accurate interactions between foreground and background elements. They introduce a scalable pipeline that improves guidance mechanisms for generating high-quality training data. The resulting dataset, named Sparkle, contains approximately 140,000 video pairs and significantly enhances the performance of models trained for this task compared to previous benchmarks.

zh.title: 视频编辑中的背景替换新突破
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个新的数据集和基准，用于视频编辑中的背景替换，解决了现有数据集的局限性。通过改进的引导机制，提出了一种可扩展的管道，能够生成高质量的前景和背景。背景替换是电影制作和广告等创意应用中的核心任务，需要合成全新的、时间一致的场景。实验表明，所提出的数据集和模型在现有基准上表现显著优于其他模型。

papers.title: EDU-CIRCUIT-HW: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models on Real-World University-Level STEM Student Handwritten Solutions
papers.abstract: Abstract EDU-CIRCUIT-HW dataset reveals significant limitations in MLLMs' ability to accurately interpret complex STEM handwritten solutions, prompting a hybrid approach combining automated recognition with minimal human oversight for improved educational grading systems.  AI-generated summary Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) hold significant promise for revolutionizing traditional education and reducing teachers' workload. However, accurately interpreting unconstrained STEM student handwritten solutions with intertwined mathematical formulas, diagrams, and textual reasoning poses a significant challenge due to the lack of authentic and domain-specific benchmarks. Additionally, current evaluation paradigms predominantly rely on the outcomes of downstream tasks (e.g., auto-grading), which often probe only a subset of the recognized content, thereby failing to capture the MLLMs' understanding of complex handwritten logic as a whole. To bridge this gap, we release EDU-CIRCUIT-HW, a dataset consisting of 1,300+ authentic student handwritten solutions from a university-level STEM course. Utilizing the expert-verified verbatim transcriptions and grading reports of student solutions, we simultaneously evaluate various MLLMs' upstream recognition fidelity and downstream auto-grading performance. Our evaluation uncovers an astonishing scale of latent failures within MLLM-recognized student handwritten content, highlighting the models' insufficient reliability for auto-grading and other understanding-oriented applications in high-stakes educational settings. As a potential solution, we present a case study demonstrating that leveraging identified error patterns to preemptively detect and correct recognition errors, while requiring only minimal human intervention (e.g., routing 3.3% of assignments to human graders and the remainder to the GPT-5.1 grader), can effectively enhance the robustness of the deployed AI-enabled grading system. Code and dataset are available in this GitHub repo: https://gt-learning-innovation.github.io/CIRCUIT_EDU_HW_ACL.

ru.title: Гибридная система оценивания STEM-решений с проверкой человеком для надёжного образовательного AI
ru.desc: В работе представлен датасет EDU-CIRCUIT-HW с более чем 1300 аутентичными рукописными решениями студентов для оценки возможностей мультимодальных больших языковых моделей в распознавании сложных STEM-задач. Исследование выявило серьёзные ограничения современных MLLM в точной интерпретации переплетённых математических формул, диаграмм и текстового рассуждения в рукописном формате. Авторы предлагают гибридный подход, сочетающий автоматическое распознавание с минимальным участием человека, что позволяет значительно улучшить надёжность систем автоматического оценивания в образовательной среде. Решение включает выявление характерных ошибок распознавания и их корректировку с привлечением экспертов только для 3,3% заданий.

en.title: Enhancing AI Grading with Minimal Human Oversight
en.desc: The EDU-CIRCUIT-HW dataset highlights the challenges faced by Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in accurately interpreting complex handwritten STEM solutions. This paper reveals that current MLLMs struggle with understanding intertwined mathematical formulas, diagrams, and textual reasoning due to a lack of suitable benchmarks. The authors propose a hybrid grading approach that combines automated recognition with minimal human oversight to improve the reliability of educational grading systems. By analyzing error patterns in MLLM outputs, the study demonstrates that targeted human intervention can significantly enhance the performance of AI in grading tasks.

zh.title: 提升教育评分系统的智能化解决方案
zh.desc: 本研究揭示了多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）在准确解读复杂STEM手写解答方面的显著局限性。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了一种混合方法，结合自动识别与最小人力监督，以改善教育评分系统。我们发布了EDU-CIRCUIT-HW数据集，包含1300多个真实学生的手写解答，并评估了不同MLLMs的识别准确性和自动评分性能。研究结果显示，MLLM在处理复杂手写逻辑时存在大量潜在失败，强调了在高风险教育环境中其可靠性不足的问题。
